task_desc: Fix frame-rate-dependent idle animation timer frequencies and minor event handler issues -- (1) three periodic idle animation timers in drawAntennae and drawLegs use the pattern `if (t - anim.timer > constant + Math.random() * range)` where Math.random() is re-rolled every frame: antenna twitch at main.js:1064 (0.8 + rand*1.2), leg jitter at main.js:1140 (1.5 + rand*2.0), wing micro-movement at main.js:1151 (2.0 + rand*3.0); at higher frame rates more random checks per second means the condition is satisfied sooner on average (e.g. after the minimum period elapses, at 120Hz there are 2x more per-frame chances for Math.random() to produce a value satisfying the threshold, so antenna twitches fire ~20% faster at 120Hz vs 60Hz); D3.2/D4.1 fixed the interpolation SPEED of these same animations using dtScale but missed the timer FREQUENCY; fix by adding anim.antennaNextInterval, anim.legJitterNextInterval, and anim.wingMicroNextInterval fields, generating a random interval once after each timer fires (e.g. anim.antennaNextInterval = 0.8 + Math.random() * 1.2), and checking deterministically with `if (t - anim.antennaTimer > anim.antennaNextInterval)` so the wait duration is wall-clock-based and frame-rate-independent, (2) window.onresize assignment at main.js:1454 uses direct property assignment instead of addEventListener('resize', resize), which overwrites any other resize handler and is fragile; replace with addEventListener, (3) handleCanvasMousemove at main.js:285 checks `activeTool !== 'air'` during active air-tool drag, so if the user switches tools mid-drag, windStrength freezes at its last value and windArrowEnd stops updating; the mouseup cleanup via dragToolOrigin (D4.1) works correctly, but mid-drag visual feedback and wind strength tracking break; similarly drawWindArrow at main.js:710 checks activeTool, hiding the arrow mid-drag; fix by checking dragToolOrigin instead of activeTool in both mousemove and drawWindArrow when isDragging is true. Files: js/main.js.
